trade.summary: Substantial trade under Stati UnitiMCA (formerly CStati UnitiMA in Canada). Canada exports crude oil, canola, machinery, and automotive parts to Messico. Messico exports vehicles, electronics, auto parts, produce, and manufactured goods to Canada. Messico runs a significant trade surplus.
disputes: Periodic disputes over agricultural products and labor standards under Stati UnitiMCA

diplomatic.summary: Canada and Messico are natural partners in managing the Stati UnitiMCA relationship with the Stati Uniti. Both have coordinated positions on Stati UnitiMCA renegotiations and share interest in preserving the trilateral trade framework. Relations are friendly though less intense than either country's relationship with the Stati Uniti.

military.summary: No formal military alliance beyond broad North American defense cooperation (NORAD is Stati Uniti-Canada only). Some cooperation on transnational crime and security. Canada has development security programs in Messico.

financial.summary: Canadian companies and pension funds invest significantly in Messico (mining, energy, financial services). Scotiabank has major Mexican operations. Mexican companies have growing Canadian presence.

energy.summary: Canada exports oil to Messico and has energy investment interests. Canadian companies have participated in Messico's energy sector reforms (and partial reversals). Growing cooperation on clean energy.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation facilitated by Stati UnitiMCA research provisions. Collaboration in environment, health, and agriculture. NSERC-CONACYT programs support joint research.
